_meta.description: City-specific zoning and development standards for SB 1123 analysis — San Diego County
_meta.usage: Keyed by city name (matching Redfin CITY field). San Diego is the baseline — other cities override where they differ.
_meta.data_gaps: See per-city 'unconfirmed' arrays for values that need verification from municipal code

setbacks._note: Gov Code 65852.28(b)(2) via 65852.21(b)(2)(B): side/rear capped at 4 ft from original lot line. Front follows local zoning. Zero between units (CBC fire separation only).

far._note: Gov Code 65852.28(b)(2)(A)(iii): state FAR floor — locals cannot impose lower.

parking._note: Gov Code 65852.28 via 65852.21(c)(1): max 1 space/unit. Zero within 0.5 mi of major transit stop (PRC 21064.3) or high-quality transit corridor (PRC 21155(b)), or within 1 block of car-share vehicle.

lot_standards._note: Gov Code 66499.41: no frontage, width, depth, or dimension requirements beyond minimum parcel SF. Locals can authorize smaller parcels.

unit_size._note: Gov Code 66499.41: average unit cannot exceed 1,750 net habitable SF (excludes garages, carports, unfinished areas).

approval._note: Gov Code 65852.28(d): ministerial, 60-day deadline, deemed approved. No CEQA (PRC 21080(b)(1)). No subjective design review. Denial only for specific adverse health/safety impact per 65589.5(d)(2). Applies to charter cities.

not_preempted.open_space: Allowed if does not conflict with FAR/density guarantee
not_preempted.height: Cannot go below existing zoning, but no state minimum height

San Diego.notes: Baseline city for SD market. Complex zoning: RS-1-{1-14} for SF (number = lot size tier), RM-{1-5}-{1-12} for MF (first number = density group, second = specific tier).

zone_mapping._note: SDMC Ch. 13, Art. 1, Div. 4. RS zones: 1-7 urbanized, 8-14 planned urbanizing. RM zones: group 1 = lowest density through 5 = highest.

height_ft._note: SDMC 131.0444. RS-1-1 to RS-1-7: 30 ft building envelope (24 ft at setback + 45° plane). RS-1-8 to RS-1-14: 35 ft flat cap. RM zones vary 30-60 ft.

height_notes.RS-1-1_to_RS-1-7: 30 ft max via building envelope: 24 ft at side setback line, then 45° angled plane inward. Effectively 2 stories with pitched roof.
height_notes.RS-1-8_to_RS-1-14: 35 ft flat height cap. Allows potential 3-story product. These are planned urbanizing area zones.
height_notes.measurement: Grade to highest point of structure.
height_notes.coastal_overlay: Coastal Height Limit Overlay (Prop D) imposes hard 30 ft cap west of I-5 (excluding downtown).

RS._note: Front varies 15-25 ft by zone. Side: min 4 ft or 10% lot width. Rear: 13 ft for primary.

setbacks_ft._notes: RS side setback: 4 ft min or 10% of lot width (lots <50 ft). RM setbacks uniform at 5 ft side.

far._note: SDMC Table 131-04J. RS FAR varies by lot area. RM FAR by zone group.
far.RS_note: FAR varies by lot area: 0.45 (>40K SF) to 0.70 (5K SF lots). See Table 131-04J.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Blue/Green/Orange lines, many bus rapid transit stops. AB 2097 eliminates parking minimums within 0.5 mi of major transit.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control in San Diego. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: City published implementation procedures (Information Bulletin 414) but has not adopted a local SB 1123 ordinance. State defaults apply with city processing guidance.
sb1123.processing_note: IB 414 outlines ministerial review process. DSD handles SB 684/1123 applications.

city_fees.school_fee_note: SDUSD charges $4.79/SF (K-12 unified district). Below state cap of $5.38.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Development Impact Fee (DIF) varies by APN — use city DIF calculator. Categories: transportation, parks, fire, library. $15K is mid-range urban infill estimate.
city_fees.notes: DIF highly variable by location — use city DIF calculator for precision. Water+sewer ~$7K/unit. Total estimate assumes mid-range urban infill.

unconfirmed: Exact DIF per unit varies by APN (use city DIF calculator)
RS-1-4 FAR table details (varies by lot area per Table 131-04J)
RS side setback formula for narrow lots
RM-4-10 and RM-5-12 detailed setback and height values
sources: SDMC Chapter 13, Art. 1, Div. 4 (Residential Base Zones) — docs.sandiego.gov/municode
City of San Diego FY 2026 Fee Schedule
City of San Diego DIF Calculator

Chula Vista.notes: Second-largest city in SD County. Uses R-1 through R-3 base zones plus PC overlays for eastern areas. Eastern area DIF among highest in county (~$39K/unit).

zone_mapping._note: CVMC Title 19. R-1 sub-zones by lot size: R-1-5 (5K SF), R-1-7 (7K), R-1-10 (10K), R-1-20 (20K), R-1-H (hillside). R-2 has R-2-P, R-2-T, R-2-X sub-zones.

height_ft._note: CVMC 19.24 (R-1), 19.26 (R-2), 19.28 (R-3). Standard R-1/R-2 = 28 ft. R-3 base = 28 ft, up to 45 ft with CUP.

height_notes.R-1_R-2: 2.5 stories or 28 ft max, whichever is less. Accessory buildings 15 ft max.
height_notes.R-3: Base 2.5 stories / 28 ft. Up to 45 ft with CUP or design review. Additional 5 ft front setback per story above 3.
height_notes.measurement: Highest point of roof line to finished grade.

setbacks_ft._notes: R-1 front varies 15-25 ft by sub-zone. R-3: 5 ft one side / 10 ft total both sides. Adjacent to R-1/R-2: R-3 side increases to 15 ft for structures over 1 story.

far._note: CVMC uses lot coverage, not FAR directly. R-1/R-2/R-3 controlled by lot coverage + density.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Blue Line stops: E St, H St, Bayfront/E St, Palomar. AB 2097 eliminates parking minimums within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: No local SB 1123 ordinance adopted. State defaults apply.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined CVESD ($2.27/SF) + SUHSD ($2.90/SF) = $5.17/SF total, at state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Eastern CV (Otay Ranch): ~$39K/unit. Western CV: much lower. $20K is blended estimate. Categories: transport, parks, fire, police, library.
city_fees.notes: Fees vary dramatically east vs west. Eastern area (Otay Ranch, Eastlake) has full DIF; western area (older urban) much lower.

unconfirmed: R-1 front setback by sub-zone (varies 15-25 ft)
Exact western vs eastern DIF per unit breakdown
R-3 FAR (code uses lot coverage not FAR)

El Cajon.notes: East County city. Uses RS zones (by lot size) for SF and RM zones (by density) for MF. Zero development impact fees — lowest-cost city in SD County for SB 1123.

zone_mapping._note: El Cajon Municipal Code Title 17. RS suffix = min lot size in thousands of SF. RM suffix = min SF per DU.

height_ft._note: ECMC Title 17 Ch. 17.140. Standard 35 ft for all residential zones.

height_notes.all: 35 ft max for all residential zones. 2.5 stories typical within 35 ft.
height_notes.near_SF: Within 100 ft of adjacent SF zone: limited to 35 ft.
height_notes.measurement: Grade to highest point of structure.

setbacks_ft._notes: RM side setback increases 5 ft per story over 2. Max 15 ft.

far._note: Lot coverage limits used. No traditional FAR specified in code.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Green Line stops at El Cajon Transit Center and Arnele Ave. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Cajon Valley Union SD + Grossmont Union HSD. At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: El Cajon has NO development impact fees — unique in SD County.
city_fees.water_fee_note: Helix Water District serves most of El Cajon
city_fees.notes: Lowest total per-unit cost in SD County. Zero DIF makes El Cajon very attractive for SB 1123.

unconfirmed: Exact setback values by zone (estimated from code structure)

La Mesa.notes: Small East County city. Uses descriptive zone names (R1E=estate through RB=business). R1/R2 height limit is very restrictive at 20 ft.

zone_mapping._note: La Mesa Municipal Code Title 24. R1E = semi-rural estate (1-2 DU/acre). R1R/R1S/R1/R1A = SF at varying densities. R2 = medium-low (1-2 family). R3 = multi-unit apartments. RB = residential-business.

height_ft._note: LMMC Title 24 Ch. 24.05. R1/R2 = 20 ft (very restrictive). R3/RB = 30 ft.

height_notes.R1_R2: 20 ft max — extremely restrictive. Essentially forces 1-story construction or very tight 2-story. SB 1123 state preemption may override but untested.
height_notes.impact: 20 ft limit is a major constraint for SB 1123 townhome product in SF zones.

setbacks_ft._notes: R1E has largest setbacks due to estate lot character.

far._note: Lot coverage used. All residential zones capped at 40%.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Orange Line stops at La Mesa Blvd, Spring St, Amaya Dr, Grossmont Transit Center. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined La Mesa-Spring Valley SD + Grossmont Union HSD. At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.water_fee_note: Helix Water District serves La Mesa
city_fees.notes: Moderate fees. DIF relatively low compared to western SD cities.

unconfirmed: Exact setback values by zone (estimated from partial code references)
sources: La Mesa-Spring Valley SD Developer Fees

Santee.notes: East County city. RS/RM zoning system. Jan 2025 fee increase (first in 19 years) made it most expensive East County city.

zone_mapping._note: Santee Municipal Code Title 13.10. RE = estate. RS by min lot size. RM-1 = low MF, RM-2 = medium MF.

height_ft._note: Santee MC 13.10. Standard 35 ft for all residential zones.

height_notes.all: 35 ft max for all residential zones. 2.5 stories typical.

setbacks_ft._notes: RS front may be 20 ft for smaller lot zones.

parking.ab2097_note: No major transit stops in Santee. Trolley Green Line terminates at Santee Town Center — check proximity.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Santee SD + Grossmont Union HSD. At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Jan 2025 increase (first in 19 years): $17.44/SF SFR, $18.39/SF MF. Most expensive East County city.
city_fees.sewer_fee_note: Padre Dam Municipal Water District serves Santee
city_fees.notes: Impact fees recently tripled. Padre Dam MWD handles water/sewer.

unconfirmed: Sewer/water capacity charges from Padre Dam MWD

Escondido.notes: North County inland city. Complex zoning with R-1 through R-5 plus R-A (agriculture) and R-T (townhouse). Development standards in Tables 33-99/100/101.

zone_mapping._note: Escondido Zoning Code Art. 33. R-1 sub-zones by min lot size (7 = 7K SF, 10 = 10K, etc.). R-2 = duplex, R-3 = apartments, R-4/R-5 = high density.

height_ft._note: Escondido Zoning Code. Standard 35 ft for R-1/R-2. R-3 up to 40 ft. R-4/R-5 up to 50 ft.

height_notes.R-1: 35 ft max. 2.5 stories typical. Additional 5 ft side setback per story above 2.
height_notes.R-3_R-4_R-5: 40-50 ft. Higher density zones allow taller structures.
height_notes.setback_note: Additional 5 ft setback on each side per story above 2, max 15 ft side setback.

setbacks_ft._notes: Tables 33-99 (front), 33-100 (side), 33-101 (rear) have detailed zone-specific setbacks. +5 ft side per story above 2.

far._note: R-3/R-4/R-5: min 70% of max density must be built (no low-density underbuilding).

parking.ab2097_note: Escondido Transit Center (Sprinter). AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Escondido Union SD + Escondido Union HSD. At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.school_district: Escondido Union SD + Escondido Union HSD
city_fees.notes: Moderate fees. Escondido has comprehensive DIF program.

sources: Escondido Zoning Code Art. 33 (Residential Zones) — ecode360.com/43265438

Poway.notes: Suburban city in north-central SD County. Predominantly single-family (RS zones). Limited multi-family. Poway Unified School District serves entire city.

zone_mapping._note: Poway Municipal Code. RR-A/B/C = rural residential by min lot size. RS-1 through RS-7 = residential single-family. RC = residential-condominium. RA = residential-apartment.

height_ft._note: Poway MC. Standard 35 ft for RS zones.

height_notes.RS: 35 ft max. 2.5 stories. Poway is predominantly single-family development.
height_notes.RA: 40 ft for apartment zone. Limited RA-zoned parcels.

setbacks_ft._notes: RS side setbacks are relatively large (10 ft) compared to other SD cities.

parking.ab2097_note: No major transit stops in Poway.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Poway Unified School District (K-12). At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.notes: Moderate-to-high fees. Poway is a suburban bedroom community with limited multi-family.

unconfirmed: All setback values (estimated from typical suburban SD standards)
RC and RA zone development standards

San Marcos.notes: North County city. Uses descriptive zone names: SFR (single-family), SFA (single-family attached), MFR (multi-family). Ch. 20.215.

zone_mapping._note: San Marcos MC Title 20 Ch. 20.215. SFR-1 through SFR-8 by density. SFA-12/18 = attached single-family. MFR-18/24/30 = multi-family by density (DU/acre).

height_ft._note: San Marcos MC Ch. 20.215. Standard 35 ft for most residential zones.

height_notes.SFR: 35 ft / 2.5 stories for all single-family zones.
height_notes.MFR: MFR-18 = 35 ft, MFR-24 = 40 ft, MFR-30 = 45 ft. Higher-density allows taller.

setbacks_ft._notes: Setbacks measured from back of right-of-way.

parking.ab2097_note: Sprinter rail stops in San Marcos (multiple). AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: San Marcos Unified School District (K-12). At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.notes: Moderate fees. Sprinter rail adds transit-oriented development potential.

unconfirmed: Exact setback values by zone from Ch. 20.215
sources: San Marcos MC Title 20 Ch. 20.215 (Residential Zones) — library.municode.com/ca/san_marcos

National City.notes: Small city between San Diego and Chula Vista. DIF fees historically very low ($4,100 since 2005), increasing to ~$15,700 effective April 2026.

zone_mapping._note: NCMC Title 18. RS = residential single-family (1=large lot, 2=small lot, 3=PD). RM = residential multi-family.

height_ft._note: NCMC Title 18. RS = 30 ft. RM varies 35-50 ft.

height_notes.RS: 2 stories / 30 ft typical for single-family zones.
height_notes.RM: Multi-family zones allow taller: RM-1 = 35 ft, RM-2 = 45 ft, RM-3 = 50 ft.

setbacks_ft._notes: Setbacks estimated. ADU setbacks confirmed: front 15 ft, side/rear 4 ft.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Blue Line stops at 8th St, 24th St, Civic Center. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined NCSD (K-6) + SUHSD (7-12). At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: New DIF effective April 20, 2026 (up from $4,100 since 2005). Per iNewsource Feb 2026.
city_fees.notes: DIF tripling from $4.1K to $15.7K effective April 2026. School fees at state cap.

unconfirmed: All height limits (estimated from typical SD County standards)

Imperial Beach.notes: Small coastal city. Zone names indicate lot area per DU. Height capped at 26 ft citywide in residential. Ongoing cross-border sewage issues.

zone_mapping._note: IBMC Title 19. Zone names = min SF per DU. R-1-6000 = 6K SF lots (SF). R-3000 = 3K SF/DU (duplex). R-2000/R-1500 = MF.

height_ft._note: IBMC Title 19. All residential zones capped at 26 ft / 2 stories.

height_notes.all: No building shall exceed 2 stories or 26 ft, whichever is less. Very restrictive — limits SB 1123 to 2-story townhomes.

setbacks_ft._notes: R-1-6000: garage front 20 ft min. Building separation: 10 ft between buildings on same lot.

parking.ab2097_note: No major transit stops in Imperial Beach. Trolley does not extend here.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance. SB 9 ordinance adopted 2023.
sb1123.notes: Small lot sizes may limit SB 1123 applicability.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined SUHSD + South Bay Union SD. At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Master Fee Schedule updated March 2024 (prior fees unchanged since 2005).
city_fees.sewer_fee_note: Cross-border Tijuana River sewage crisis may affect future fees.
city_fees.notes: Fees relatively low but sewer situation complicated by cross-border sewage. Master fee schedule updated 2024.

unconfirmed: All FAR/lot coverage values (code may use lot coverage instead of FAR)

Coronado.notes: Affluent island/peninsula city. Restrictive zoning with daylight plane rules and aggressive FAR limits. Active resistance to state housing mandates. Affordable housing in-lieu fee ($25/SF) is major cost driver.

zone_mapping._note: CMC Title 86. R-1A = standard SF (5,250-7,500 SF lots). R-1B = small-lot SF (3,500 SF). R-3 = medium MF. R-5 = high-density MF (47 DU/AC).

height_ft._note: CMC 86.08.030 (R-1A), 86.10.030 (R-1B). Height depends on roof pitch. Daylight plane from 18 ft at side property line at 45 degrees.

height_notes.R-1A: Flat/mansard or <3:12 pitch: 23 ft. 3:12 to <6:12: 27.5 ft. 6:12+ pitch: 30 ft.
height_notes.daylight_plane: 18 ft starting height at side property line, 45° incline inward. Constrains upper-story width.
height_notes.R-3: 35 ft for sloped roof (2:12+). Design review required for 3+ units.
height_notes.R-5: 50 ft / 5 stories for designated housing element sites.

setbacks_ft._notes: R-1A rear: 25 ft ground floor; 2nd story to 40% of lot depth. R-3 rear steps back with height.

far.R-1A_note: Base 0.475, up to 0.595 with approved design features.

parking.R-3_note: 3 spaces per unit on lots 5,600+ SF; 2 enclosed. Very high parking requirement.
parking.ab2097_note: No major transit stops in Coronado. Ferry terminal may not qualify.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. City adversarial toward state housing mandates.
sb1123.notes: HCD issued findings Dec 2025 that Coronado's ADU ordinance violates state law. Very few vacant SF-zoned lots exist.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Coronado Unified School District (K-12). At state Level 1 cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: RTCIP fee: $3,047/unit (FY 2025-26). Relatively low base DIF.
city_fees.affordable_housing_note: In-lieu fee: $25/SF effective 2025, rising to $35/SF (2027), $45/SF (2028), $55/SF (2029). On 1,200 SF unit = $30,000.
city_fees.notes: Affordable housing in-lieu fee ($25/SF = $30K on 1,200 SF unit) is the dominant cost. Total fees very high. May apply to SB 1123 projects.

unconfirmed: Whether affordable housing in-lieu fee applies to SB 1123 projects
sources: Coronado Times (Jul 2025) — affordable housing in-lieu fee phasing

Lemon Grove.notes: Small inland city east of San Diego. Uses RL/RM zone system. Very restrictive 25 ft height limit across all zones.

zone_mapping._note: LGMC Title 17. RL = low density SF. RL/M = small lot SF. RM = medium density (duplex/townhouse). RM/H = medium-high density (garden apartments).

height_ft._note: LGMC Title 17. All residential zones 25 ft max for main buildings. Very restrictive.

height_notes.all: 25 ft main buildings, 15 ft accessory across all residential zones. Effectively limits to 2 stories.
height_notes.RM_H: 25 ft even for medium-high density. Planned development permit required in RM/H.
height_notes.impact: SB 1123 state preemption may override local 25 ft cap.

setbacks_ft._notes: RL has very large setbacks. RL/M, RM, RM/H may waive interior side/rear for zero-lot-line with planned development permit.

far._note: Uses open space minimums, not FAR. RL: 2,000 SF usable open space/DU. RM/H: 500 SF/DU.

parking.ab2097_note: Trolley Orange Line stops at Lemon Grove Depot and Massachusetts Ave. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined LGSD (K-8) + GUHSD (9-12). Fee split to stay at state cap.
city_fees.water_fee_note: Helix Water District serves most of Lemon Grove
city_fees.notes: Relatively affordable fees. Small city with limited infrastructure costs.

unconfirmed: FAR values (code may not specify FAR)
Sewer capacity fee (Padre Dam or City)

Carlsbad.notes: North County coastal city. Zones: R-1 through R-3 + RD-M. Comprehensive impact fee program with inclusionary housing ordinance (15% affordable).

zone_mapping._note: CMC Title 21. R-1 = single family. R-2 = two-family. R-3 = multiple family. RD-M = residential density-multiple (higher density).

height_ft._note: CMC Title 21. R-1/R-2: 30 ft (pitched roof) / 24 ft (flat). R-3/RD-M: 35 ft.

height_notes.R-1_R-2: 30 ft for pitched roof, 24 ft for flat roof. 2 stories max.

far._note: Not explicitly stated for most zones. Lot coverage controlled by zone.

parking.ab2097_note: Coaster stations at Carlsbad Village and Carlsbad Poinsettia. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Carlsbad Unified School District. Increased June 2024. Near state cap.
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Comprehensive fee program: traffic, park in-lieu, drainage, sewer capacity, water capacity.
city_fees.affordable_housing_note: Inclusionary housing ordinance: 15% affordable or in-lieu fee.
city_fees.notes: Among highest development fees in North County SD. Master Fee Schedule updated annually.

unconfirmed: R-1 specific FAR / lot coverage percentage
Exact per-unit breakdown of impact fees
School fee rate (CUSD website says increased June 2024)
Water and sewer capacity charges per EDU

Encinitas.notes: Proposition A (June 2013) defines this city: 30 ft / 2-story citywide height limit, voter approval for density increases. Affordable housing in-lieu fee ($25.15/SF) is major cost.

zone_mapping._note: EMC Title 30. Zones named by density (DU/acre). RR/R-3/R-5/R-8/RS-11 = SF. R-11/R-15/R-20/R-25/R-30 = higher density/MF.

height_ft._note: EMC 30.16.010. Prop A limits citywide to 30 ft / 2 stories (voter-approved).

height_notes.prop_a: Proposition A (June 2013) imposes citywide 30 ft / 2-story height limit. Any zoning change increasing density or height requires voter approval.
height_notes.R-30: R-30 Overlay: 35 ft flat roof, 39 ft pitched. Only zone exceeding 30 ft limit.
height_notes.prop_a_history: Prop A blocked Housing Element upzoning twice (Measure T 2016, Measure U 2018). Judge eventually let Council bypass voters.

setbacks_ft._notes: Low-density SF zones have larger setbacks. Garage front = 20 ft min.

far._note: Encinitas does not use traditional FAR for most residential zones. Density controlled by lot area per DU.

parking.ab2097_note: Coaster stations at Encinitas and Cardiff. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. Prop A voter approval may complicate any local SB 1123 implementation requiring zoning changes.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Encinitas Union Elementary ($2.45/SF) + San Dieguito Union HSD ($2.72/SF) = $5.17/SF.
city_fees.school_district: Encinitas Union ESD + San Dieguito Union HSD
city_fees.impact_fees_note: Park, traffic, fire, flood, recreation trails, community facilities, open space.
city_fees.affordable_housing_note: In-lieu fee: $25.15/SF effective July 2025 (up from $20/SF). ~$30K+ on 1,200 SF unit. Major cost factor.
city_fees.notes: High development fees. Affordable housing in-lieu fee significant. Impact fees waived for ADUs except sewer (>750 SF) and school (>500 SF).

unconfirmed: FAR values (may not use FAR for residential)
Whether affordable housing in-lieu fee applies to SB 1123
sources: Encinitas Union ESD Developer Fee Study 2024

Del Mar.notes: Extremely restrictive small city (~1,800 homes). 26 ft height, Design Review required, Coastal Commission overlay. SB 1123 opportunities very limited.

zone_mapping._note: DMMC Title 30. Zone numbers = min lot size in thousands of SF. R1-40 = 40K SF, R1-14 = 14K, R1-10 = 10K. Very small city.

height_ft._note: DMMC Title 30. 26 ft / 2 stories standard. Most height-restrictive in SD County.

height_notes.all: 26 ft / 2 stories max throughout residential zones. Most height-restrictive city in SD County.
height_notes.downtown: Downtown Initiative (Measure B): west side of Camino del Mar limited to 14 ft above road grade.
height_notes.coastal: Nearly all of Del Mar is in Coastal Zone — California Coastal Commission review may apply.

parking.ab2097_note: Amtrak/Coaster station exists. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi of station.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. Practically very limited: Design Review required, Coastal Commission, few MF-zoned lots.
sb1123.practical_note: SB 1123 opportunities extremely limited in Del Mar.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Del Mar Union ESD + San Dieguito Union HSD ($2.72/SF).
city_fees.school_district: Del Mar Union ESD + San Dieguito Union HSD
city_fees.notes: Small city, high-cost. Design Review adds time/expense. Fee schedule updated annually.

unconfirmed: All setback values (estimated from zone names and coastal comparables)
All fee amounts (Del Mar fee PDFs not accessible)
Del Mar Union ESD school fee rate
sources: Del Mar City Fee Schedules — delmar.ca.us

Solana Beach.notes: Small coastal city. 25 ft height limit across all zones (most restrictive in region). Unique setback designator system (A-D on zoning map). Only 1 parking space per unit.

zone_mapping._note: SBMC Ch. 17.20. Uses descriptive names with setback designators (A-D) on zoning map.

height_ft._note: SBMC 17.20.050. All residential 25 ft base. MHR/HR can increase to 30 ft with development review.

height_notes.all: 25 ft max for all residential zones, subject to daylight plane limitation.
height_notes.MHR_HR: MHR and HR zones may increase to 30 ft with Development Review Permit.
height_notes.second_story: Second stories shall be set back 15 ft from street right-of-way.

setbacks_ft._note: Determined by setback designator (A-D) on zoning map. Values below are simplified for pro forma.

far._note: SBMC 17.20.060. SF zones use graduated FAR. MF zones use flat 0.75.

parking.per_unit_note: SBMC 17.20.070. Only 1 off-street space per residential unit — lowest in region.
parking.ab2097_note: Coaster station in Solana Beach. AB 2097 applies within 0.5 mi.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. No local SB 1123 ordinance.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Combined Solana Beach ESD + San Dieguito Union HSD ($2.72/SF).
city_fees.school_district: Solana Beach ESD + San Dieguito Union HSD
city_fees.notes: Fee schedule effective March 17, 2025. Park fee ($7,445/unit) is largest single impact fee.

unconfirmed: Exact Solana Beach ESD school fee rate
Sewer connection fee vs annual rate ($750 may be annual EDU rate)

Unincorporated SD County.notes: Covers Spring Valley, Ramona, Julian, Lakeside, Jamul, Alpine, Rancho Santa Fe, Bonita, etc. Complex designator system: zone-density-setback (e.g., RS-1-4). Fees vary enormously by location and serving utility district.

zone_mapping._note: SD County Zoning Ordinance (Ord. 5281, Title 8). Designators: zone-density-setback (e.g., RS-1-4). RS = single family, RR = rural, RV = variable, RM = multiple, RC = residential-commercial.

height_ft._note: County Zoning Ordinance Sec. 4300/4310. Height designated by letter on zoning map. 'B' = 35 ft (typical residential).

height_notes.note: Actual height varies by specific designator on property's zoning map.

setbacks_ft._note: County Zoning Ordinance Sec. 4810. Setbacks designated by number (1-10+) on zoning map.

parking.ab2097_note: Limited transit in unincorporated areas. Trolley stops near Spring Valley / La Mesa border.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 applies.

sb1123.status: State defaults apply. County has not adopted local SB 1123 ordinance.
sb1123.notes: Grand Jury report (2024-25) found development impact fees are barrier to housing in unincorporated areas.

city_fees.school_fee_note: Varies by school district. Multiple districts serve unincorporated areas. State cap $5.38.
city_fees.school_district: Various: San Diego Unified, Grossmont Union HSD, Ramona USD, Julian Union, Mountain Empire USD
city_fees.impact_fees_note: County Transportation Impact Fee (TIF) varies by Community Planning Area. Ranges $3K-$15K per unit.
city_fees.sewer_fee_note: Varies: some areas on septic. County sewer/water or SD city services (~$3K-$5K per EDU).
city_fees.notes: Fees vary enormously by location. Spring Valley near SD city services; Julian/Ramona on septic/well. TIF varies by community plan area.

unconfirmed: All setback values (County uses designator system; typical values estimated)
Height designator values (35 ft assumed for RS)
All fee amounts (complex, vary by community plan area)
sources: SD County Zoning Ordinance (Ord. 5281) Title 8 — sandiegocounty.gov/pds/zoning.html
SD County Grand Jury Report 2024-25 on Development Impact Fees
